articles.title: Respite from heatwave as heavy rain to lash Odisha till June 22, low-pressure likely next week - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast widespread rainfall across Odisha till June 22, with heavy rain, thunderstorms, lightning and strong winds expected in several districts over the next few days.
The weather warning comes at a time when several districts have already witnessed lightning-related deaths and rain-triggered incidents over the past few days. The IMD has urged people to remain cautious during thunderstorms and avoid venturing into open areas during lightning activity.
Orange Warning For 7 Districts
The Regional Meteorological Centre has issued an Orange Warning for seven districts on Thursday. Koraput, Rayagada and Gajapati are likely to witness thunderstorms accompanied by gusty winds reaching 50 to 60 kmph.
The remaining districts have been placed under Yellow Warning for thunderstorms, lightning and moderate rainfall.
The weather office has also warned of heavy rainfall in parts of southern Odisha, with rain activity expected to intensify over the next 48 hours.
Entire State On Alert tomorrow
According to the forecast, all districts of Odisha will remain under weather alert on Friday.
Malkangiri, Koraput, Mayurbhanj and Balasore are likely to receive heavy rainfall, while several other districts may witness thunderstorms, lightning and strong surface winds.
Officials have advised district administrations to remain prepared for localised waterlogging, uprooting of trees, damage to weak structures and traffic disruptions in areas receiving intense rainfall.
Relief From Heat, But Humidity To Continue
The rainfall is expected to provide some relief from the hot and humid conditions prevailing across several parts of the state, especially in coastal districts.
However, weather experts say humidity levels are likely to remain high during the active monsoon phase, meaning people may continue to experience sticky and uncomfortable conditions despite a drop in temperature.
Low-Pressure Likely Around June 24-25
Meanwhile, the IMD has indicated the possibility of a low-pressure area forming over the north Bay of Bengal and adjoining Odisha coast around June 24-25.
If the system develops as predicted, it could further enhance rainfall activity across Odisha and influence weather conditions during the last week of June.
The Meteorological Centre said it is closely monitoring the situation and will issue further updates as the system evolves.

articles.title: Heatwave conditions: Class time for school students across Balasore district revised, check details - KalingaTV
articles.full_text:
Heatwave conditions: Class time for school students across Balasore district revised, check details
Balasore district administration has revised the class time of all schools in the district in view of the increasing heatwave
Balasore: In view of the increasing heatwave and humid conditions in the district and based on the concerns regarding the safety and health of the students, the Balasore district administration has revised the class time of all schools in the district.
As per the announcement made by the District Collector, class time of all educational institutions (primary, secondary and higher secondary), government/aided/private (including KV/JNV/OAVs), in the district have been revised and will be held between 6.30 AM and 10.30 AM.
This will be effective from today until further orders. The weather conditions will be reviewed from time to time and fresh orders will be issued as and when required, informed the district collector’s office.
Enrollment and mid-day meal in schools will continue as normal during the MDM time frame.
However, the timings of examinations and BLO/SIR work to be done by teachers will remain unchanged and will continue with all precautionary measures as per the previously decided schedule.
